questions.q: Kaikeyi was which wife of King Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was Dasharatha's second queen, alongside Kausalya and Sumitra.

questions.q: Who was Kaikeyi's son?
questions.explanation: Bharata, who later ruled Ayodhya as regent in Rama's name, was Kaikeyi's son.

questions.q: Which scheming maidservant turned Kaikeyi against Rama?
questions.explanation: Manthara, Kaikeyi's hunchbacked maid, poisoned her mind against Rama's coronation.

questions.q: How many boons did Kaikeyi demand from Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi invoked two boons Dasharatha had earlier promised her.

questions.q: What did Kaikeyi demand happen to Rama with one of her boons?
options: That he be exiled to the forest
questions.explanation: One boon demanded Rama's exile to the forest for fourteen years.

questions.q: With her second boon, whom did Kaikeyi want placed on the throne of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's second boon demanded that her son Bharata be made king.

questions.q: For how many years did Kaikeyi demand Rama be exiled?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded Rama live in the forest for fourteen years.

questions.q: What was Kaikeyi's title or epithet on this card?
questions.explanation: The card titles Kaikeyi 'The Queen Who Asked,' referring to her two fateful boons.

questions.q: Whose exile resulted directly from Kaikeyi's demands?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's boons sent Rama into a fourteen-year forest exile.

questions.q: In which kingdom was Kaikeyi a queen?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was one of the queens of Ayodhya, ruled by King Dasharatha.

questions.q: Kaikeyi was the daughter of the king of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was the princess of the Kekaya kingdom, from which her name derives.

questions.q: On what occasion did Dasharatha originally promise Kaikeyi the two boons?
options: After she saved his life in a battle
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi tended Dasharatha after he was wounded in a battle aiding the gods, and he granted her two boons.

questions.q: Where did Kaikeyi retreat to make her demands, refusing comfort?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi withdrew to the 'kopa-bhavana,' the chamber of wrath, lying on the bare floor to press her demands.

questions.q: How did Dasharatha react when Kaikeyi insisted on her two boons?
options: He fainted and was overcome with grief
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was so devastated by Kaikeyi's demands that he collapsed in grief and despair.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Kaikeyi made her demands and Rama left for exile?
options: Away visiting his maternal grandfather's kingdom
questions.explanation: Bharata was away at the Kekaya kingdom of his maternal grandfather and knew nothing of the plot.

questions.q: What was Bharata's reaction when he returned and learned what his mother had done?
options: He was furious and condemned Kaikeyi
He exiled himself willingly with joy
questions.explanation: Bharata was horrified and angrily rebuked Kaikeyi, refusing to accept the throne won by such treachery.

questions.q: What fear did Manthara plant in Kaikeyi about her future if Rama became king?
options: That she and Bharata would be subservient to Kausalya and Rama
That Lakshmana would seize the army
questions.explanation: Manthara warned that under Rama's rule, Kaikeyi would become a servant to Kausalya and Bharata would be sidelined.

questions.q: Who accompanied Rama into exile after Kaikeyi's boons took effect?
questions.explanation: Sita and Lakshmana voluntarily followed Rama into his forest exile.

questions.q: What happened to Dasharatha not long after Rama departed for the forest?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha died of heartbreak soon after Rama left, unable to bear separation from his beloved son.

questions.q: When Bharata went to the forest to bring Rama back, what did he ultimately do upon Rama's refusal?
options: He seized the crown for himself
He ruled as regent, placing Rama's sandals on the throne
He joined Rama in exile permanently
questions.explanation: Bharata installed Rama's sandals (paduka) on the throne and ruled as a regent in Rama's name.

questions.q: From which town or place did Bharata rule as regent during Rama's exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed from Nandigrama, living austerely and keeping Rama's sandals on the throne.

questions.q: How is Kaikeyi generally regarded in tradition after these events?
options: As a heroine who saved Ayodhya
As the figure whose demands set the epic's tragedy in motion
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi is remembered as the queen whose boons triggered Rama's exile and the epic's central conflict.

questions.q: According to tradition, on whose side did Dasharatha fight the battle during which Kaikeyi earned her boons?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha aided the gods (devas) in their war against the demons, and was wounded, after which Kaikeyi saved him.

questions.q: What specific act of Kaikeyi during the battle is traditionally said to have saved Dasharatha's life?
options: She healed his wounds with herbs
She steadied a broken chariot axle, some say with her own hand
She carried him from the field on horseback
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Kaikeyi acted as his charioteer and saved him when his chariot faltered, earning the two boons.

questions.q: By what other name, derived from her father, is Kaikeyi sometimes referred to in the Ramayana?
options: Kaikeyi (daughter of the Kekaya king Ashvapati)
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was the daughter of Ashvapati, king of Kekaya; her name marks her as the Kekaya princess.

questions.q: What promise concerning succession is sometimes said to have been part of Kaikeyi's marriage agreement with Dasharatha?
options: That her son would inherit the throne
That she would never bear children
That Rama would be banished from birth
questions.explanation: In some traditions, Kaikeyi's father secured a pledge that her son would succeed Dasharatha, which Manthara later invoked.

questions.q: Whom did Bharata blame and treat with contempt as the instigator of the whole scheme?
questions.explanation: Bharata held Manthara responsible for poisoning Kaikeyi's mind and treated her with scorn.

questions.q: After Rama returned and was crowned, how is Kaikeyi's later relationship with him generally described?
options: Rama forgave her and treated her with respect
Rama never spoke to her again
questions.explanation: Rama, ever dutiful, forgave Kaikeyi and continued to honor her as one of his fathers' queens.

questions.q: What did Kaikeyi initially feel when Manthara first brought her news of Rama's planned coronation, before being persuaded otherwise?
options: Joy, and she rewarded the messenger
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was at first delighted at the news of Rama's coronation and even gave Manthara a gift before being swayed.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the events caused by Kaikeyi's boons primarily unfold in which book (kanda)?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's demands and Rama's departure for exile are central to the Ayodhya Kanda, the second book of the epic.
